title: Kaworu is Shinjis clone
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu is Shinji's clone? Post by doubletree » Fri Sep 11, 2009 7:40 pm Hello everyone. I am a relatively new Eva Geek drawn here by my ongoing interest (or, you could say, obsession) with the show. I do not, like many of you do I'm sure, have an encyclopedic knowledge of the history of online Eva debates, and I've only recently started learning about some of the Eva apocrypha, etc. However, there's one thing I wanted to throw out there since I have not yet seen any mention of it and it appears to my eyes to be fairly obvious from my point of view (although of course I welcome debate intended to dissuade me from my opinion!). Kaworu is Shinji's clone. Evidence: We know from episode 21 that some DNA mysteriously 'dived' into Adam on the day of 2nd impact. Who's DNA was it? We have no clue. But we do know that Kaworu was born on that day as a vessel for the soul of Adam. There is another example of a character with the soul of a 'god' that lives in the cloned body of a human. Any guesses? You got it. Rei. The soul of Lilith in the cloned body of Yui. This strange mish-mash was engineered by the big-hearted Gendo Ikari. Gendo was a member of the research team. It's possible that he was in the position to orchestrate whose DNA was going to be used for the experiment. Kaworu is the seventeenth angel. He has some strange fascination with Shinji that is never fully explained in the series. He eventually decides to yield his angelic agenda to allow humanity to continue to exist. Why? He's literally the same person as Shinji, with a different soul. A closer look at the evidence might yield more information that could either support or disprove this theory. But here's what I consider to be the smoking gun: In episode 24, in the final scene, Shinji mentions that he was "like" me : "Kaoru-kun said 'I love you.' He looked like me ["was like me"]. And Ayanami. I loved him. Kaoru-kun should have been the one to survive." (Note that this is from my translation, see the correction below) Kaworu's resemblance to Ayanami is apparently due to the family relationship, of sorts, that would exist in this case between the three in this case. This also appears to be confirmed by the fact that Kaworu refers to Gendo in Evangelion 2.0 (for those of you who have seen the crappy camrip) as his 'father.' As far as I know, this theory may already be accepted, rejected, proven, disproven, or massively already puzzled upon in the world of Eva geeks. What do you think?

[]: Re: Kaworu is Shinji's clone? Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Sep 11, 2009 8:12 pm doubletree wrote: We know from episode 21 that some DNA mysteriously 'dived' into Adam on the day of 2nd impact. Who's DNA was it? We have no clue. But we do know that Kaworu was born on that day as a vessel for the soul of Adam.

[]: Post by FallenTabris » Fri Sep 11, 2009 8:20 pm "In episode 24, in the final scene, Shinji mentions that he "looked like me:" I think this is the phrase you're referring to 僕に似てたんだ This phrase is more like "(he) was just like me". I think he means it in an emotional sense, but certainly not in one of physical appearance.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Sep 11, 2009 8:34 pm Um. Shinji wasn't even born yet when Second Impact, Kaworu's birthdate, happened. So fail. If anything, he's a clone of Gendo (which is still the best theory ever stfu).

[]: Post by doubletree » Fri Sep 11, 2009 8:35 pm FallenTabris wrote: "In episode 24, in the final scene, Shinji mentions that he "looked like me:" I think this is the phrase you're referring to 僕に似てたんだ This phrase is more like "(he) was just like me". I think he means it in an emotional sense, but certainly not in one of physical appearance.

[]: Post by Fazmotron » Fri Sep 11, 2009 9:59 pm No, just no. They look nothing alike, their clothes are the same because its a school uniform, and Shinji was born AFTER Kaworu. Nice idea, but there are no facts to back it up.

[]: Post by doubletree » Fri Sep 11, 2009 10:28 pm Fazmotron wrote: No, just no. They look nothing alike, their clothes are the same because its a school uniform, and Shinji was born AFTER Kaworu. Nice idea, but there are no facts to back it up.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Fri Sep 11, 2009 10:35 pm doubletree wrote: They look like two slightly altered drawings of the same person.

[]: Post by doubletree » Fri Sep 11, 2009 10:39 pm esselfortium wrote: doubletree wrote: They look like two slightly altered drawings of the same person.

[]: Post by Fazmotron » Fri Sep 11, 2009 10:52 pm The features you describe can be attributed to every character in Evangelion. The similarity is there because they are all drawn by the same artist. Apart from the superficial similarities in character styling, there is very little similarity in appearance between Shinji and Kaworu. I suggest that next time you have a theory, try to support it with more than just appearances.

[]: Post by doubletree » Fri Sep 11, 2009 11:14 pm Fazmotron wrote: The features you describe can be attributed to every character in Evangelion. The similarity is there because they are all drawn by the same artist. Apart from the superficial similarities in character styling, there is very little similarity in appearance between Shinji and Kaworu. I suggest that next time you have a theory, try to support it with more than just appearances.

[]: Post by doubletree » Fri Sep 11, 2009 11:23 pm Notice also that Yui and Rei Both have the pointy nose:

[]: Post by Ornette » Fri Sep 11, 2009 11:25 pm Kaworu was also "like Ayanami", does that make him a clone of her?

[]: Post by doubletree » Fri Sep 11, 2009 11:35 pm Ornette wrote: Kaworu was also "like Ayanami", does that make him a clone of her?

[]: Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Sep 11, 2009 11:55 pm doubletree wrote: but not the money to give him an appearance at least remotely unique from the main character of the story? That seems unlikely.

[]: Post by Ornette » Sat Sep 12, 2009 12:03 am Lots of pointy noses in Shinji's class: Looks pretty pointy to me. Lots of pointy noses here. This looks extremely pointy. So do these. In comparison, showing that the pointiness of noses depends on which artist is doing the drawing. This doesn't look extremely pointy.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sat Sep 12, 2009 12:04 am New theory: every character we ever see in the entirety of NGE, no matter how minor, is actually a clone of Shinji. The Rei tank was just a red herring! edit: Better yet, every character is a clone of Shinji's nose, specifically. Make up your own ideas about where the other parts of them came from.

[]: Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sat Sep 12, 2009 12:09 am @Ornette: Its just my personal opinion but maybe instead of shipping or at least the usual shipps I think Shinji would be better off forgetting Rei or Asuka and maybe focusing on one of his many fangirls after they found out he was the pilot in your second picture.

[]: Post by doubletree » Sat Sep 12, 2009 12:12 am The facts remain, however: 1) According to the timeline, there is a fairly good possibility that Shinji would have already been conceived when the research team 'dived' the DNA into Adam and produced Kaworu. Kaworu is definitely cloned from someone . 2) Kaworu seeks out Shinji almost immediately after arriving at Nerv, for an unknown reason, and immediately and without provokation develops an extremely intense relationship with him. 3) Although there are a plethora of other very pointed-nosed female characters in the story, Kaworu and Shinji are the only two primary male characters who share the long and pointy nose. In other words, I am referring primarily to the llegnth of the nose, not the angle. I see this as a Ikari family trait from Yui's side, but considering the number of pointy noses in the series, I guess we'll just have to leave this one up to personal judgement. 4) Both have a very slim body type, and thin neck types. In other words, both are unusually effeminate looking male characters due to their genetic traits. 5) In Evangelion 2.0, SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by Ornette » Sat Sep 12, 2009 1:17 am doubletree wrote: The facts remain, however: 1) According to the timeline, there is a fairly good possibility that Shinji would have already been conceived when the research team 'dived' the DNA into Adam and produced Kaworu. Kaworu is definitely cloned from someone .

[]: Kaworu is Shinji's clone? Post by doubletree » Fri Sep 11, 2009 7:40 pm Hello everyone. I am a relatively new Eva Geek drawn here by my ongoing interest (or, you could say, obsession) with the show. I do not, like many of you do I'm sure, have an encyclopedic knowledge of the history of online Eva debates, and I've only recently started learning about some of the Eva apocrypha, etc. However, there's one thing I wanted to throw out there since I have not yet seen any mention of it and it appears to my eyes to be fairly obvious from my point of view (although of course I welcome debate intended to dissuade me from my opinion!). Kaworu is Shinji's clone. Evidence: We know from episode 21 that some DNA mysteriously 'dived' into Adam on the day of 2nd impact. Who's DNA was it? We have no clue. But we do know that Kaworu was born on that day as a vessel for the soul of Adam. There is another example of a character with the soul of a 'god' that lives in the cloned body of a human. Any guesses? You got it. Rei. The soul of Lilith in the cloned body of Yui. This strange mish-mash was engineered by the big-hearted Gendo Ikari. Gendo was a member of the research team. It's possible that he was in the position to orchestrate whose DNA was going to be used for the experiment. Kaworu is the seventeenth angel. He has some strange fascination with Shinji that is never fully explained in the series. He eventually decides to yield his angelic agenda to allow humanity to continue to exist. Why? He's literally the same person as Shinji, with a different soul. A closer look at the evidence might yield more information that could either support or disprove this theory. But here's what I consider to be the smoking gun: In episode 24, in the final scene, Shinji mentions that he was "like" me : "Kaoru-kun said 'I love you.' He looked like me ["was like me"]. And Ayanami. I loved him. Kaoru-kun should have been the one to survive."
